ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a term for 'chicken nugget' (FOODON_03302050) which covers the McNuggets component. No single FoodOn term covers the combined McNuggets + chicken wings sharing box; closest_parent match applied to the primary component. McWings (bone-in fried chicken wings) are not separately coded in FoodOn at this time.
ontology.foodex2_description: Chicken meat products — breaded/battered fried chicken
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC (chicken meat products) is the closest defensible broader category for this combined fried chicken product set. No single FoodEx2 code covers a mixed McNuggets + McWings sharing box. Closest_parent match applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT food concept found that specifically covers this combined fast-food sharing box product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One Happy Sharing Box A contains 12 Chicken McNuggets and 8 McWings, estimated at approximately 560 g total, intended as a shared meal for 2–3 people.

nutrient_source.title: McDonald's Singapore Nutrition Information — Chicken McNuggets and McWings
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (Foundation Foods / Branded Foods) — used as primary reference for fried breaded chicken nuggets and fried chicken wings; cross-referenced with McDonald's Singapore published nutrition data
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: McDonald's Singapore does not publish per-100g nutrient data publicly in a machine-readable approved-source format. Nutrient values are estimated by combining USDA FoodData Central data for (1) breaded fried chicken nuggets (USDA FDC ID 171477, 'Chicken nuggets, frozen, unprepared' and related branded entries) and (2) fried chicken wings (USDA FDC SR Legacy item 05062, 'Chicken, wing, meat and skin, cooked, fried, batter'). The combined per-100g profile is a weighted average reflecting the approximate mass ratio of McNuggets (~55%) to McWings (~45%) in the sharing box. This is an adapted/estimated match. Confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; and University of Sydney GI Database analogous food: battered/breaded fried chicken
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal (peer-reviewed)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for McDonald's Happy Sharing Box A. GI of 66 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for breaded/battered fried chicken products (analogous food: chicken nuggets / fried battered chicken, GI ~66, medium category), as reported in Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283). The carbohydrate contribution is primarily from the wheat-flour breading. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (66 × 75.6) / 100 ≈ 49.9, where carbohydrate per serving = 13.5 g/100g × 560 g serving = 75.6 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~480 mg per 100 g; ~2,688 mg per full sharing box serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this product, as a single sharing box provides approximately 112% of the WHO recommended daily sodium limit of 2,000 mg, primarily from seasoning, breading, and processing additives in both the McNuggets and McWings.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (GI 66), high glycemic load per serving (~50), and ultra-processed formulation with refined wheat-flour breading
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this product as an occasional indulgence only; the high glycemic load of approximately 50 per sharing box, combined with the high fat content, can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation and impair insulin sensitivity, and portion control with pairing alongside non-starchy vegetables is strongly advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat (16.2 g/100g), saturated fat (3.5 g/100g), trans fat (0.1 g/100g), cholesterol (58 mg/100g), and very high sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should minimise consumption of this product due to its combination of saturated fat, trans fat, cholesterol, and very high sodium, all of which are independently associated with elevated LDL cholesterol, arterial inflammation, and increased cardiovascular event risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: McDonald's Happy Sharing Box A is a fast-food sharing platter available at McDonald's Singapore, consisting of 12 pieces of Chicken McNuggets and 8 pieces of McWings, designed for sharing among 2 to 3 people. Chicken McNuggets are bite-sized pieces of minced and formed white chicken breast meat coated in a light seasoned wheat-flour batter and deep-fried until golden and crispy. McWings are bone-in chicken wing sections (drumette and flat) marinated in a spiced coating and deep-fried to a crispy finish. Both products are ultra-processed, using modified starches, sodium phosphates, and autolyzed yeast extract in their formulations. The combined product is certified Halal in Singapore. Nutritionally, the sharing box is calorie-dense at approximately 267 kcal per 100 g, with a full box estimated at around 1,495 kcal. It is high in protein (~16.8 g/100g), high in total fat (~16.2 g/100g including saturated and small amounts of trans fat), and moderately high in refined carbohydrates from the breading (~13.5 g/100g). Sodium content is very high at approximately 480 mg per 100 g, making the full box a significant sodium load of roughly 2,688 mg — exceeding the WHO daily recommended limit. The glycemic index is estimated at 66 (medium) based on analogous breaded fried chicken data from the University of Sydney GI Database, with a high glycemic load per serving of approximately 50. Clinically, this product is of high concern for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and cardiovascular disease due to its sodium, saturated fat, trans fat, and glycemic load profile. It is best consumed occasionally and in moderation, ideally paired with lower-GI, higher-fibre foods.

confidence_reason: No exact per-100g nutrient data for this specific McDonald's Singapore product is available from an approved primary source (HPB Foodo, USDA FoodData Central branded entry for this exact SKU, or ICMR-NIN). Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central data for breaded fried chicken nuggets and fried chicken wings, weighted by approximate mass ratio. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for breaded/battered fried chicken. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are a weighted-average estimate combining USDA FDC data for breaded fried chicken nuggets (~55% mass) and fried chicken wings (~45% mass); exact McDonald's Singapore formulation data is not publicly available in an approved-source format. 2. Serving size of 560 g is estimated: 12 McNuggets × ~19 g each = ~228 g; 8 McWings × ~41 g each = ~328 g; total ~556 g, rounded to 560 g. 3. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entries used for this estimation. 4. Trans fat (0.1 g/100g) is a small residual amount from frying oils; confirmed as trace level in USDA FDC data for similar fried chicken products. 5. Vitamin C is reported as 0.0 g/100g; this is confirmed zero per USDA FDC data for fried breaded chicken — fried chicken products contain no meaningful vitamin C. 6. GI of 66 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this exact McDonald's product was found in the University of Sydney GI Database or peer-reviewed literature. 7. The product is listed as Halal-certified at McDonald's Singapore. 8. Price data from field survey data: SGD 16.70 (fixed price across 2 outlets observed). 9. This record covers the combined sharing box as a single food entity; individual McNuggets and McWings records may be created separately if needed.
